next_steps: Produce a candidate object_detect ONNX via #7b-train (currently blocked: training set requires >=50 manual_corrected rows; reviewer accumulation has not started, manual_corrected_total=0).

Once a candidate ONNX exists: rerun eval_pr.py and eval_distribution.py with the candidate model loaded -> obtain face/tool candidate reports -> rerun shadow_eval.py --from-reports for the real candidate-vs-current verdict.
Live mode (without --from-reports) is also available; orchestrator handles the eval_distribution.py rerun internally given a candidate ONNX path.
non_goals: NOT a candidate-vs-current comparison; both inputs are identical.
NOT a #5-bootstrap retrain trigger; gate (>=50 manual_corrected rows) remains unsatisfied.
NOT an eval_pr.py rerun against the live server; reuses the existing 2c-recognition-pr.json artifact.
reproducer: python3 tools/shadow_eval.py --from-reports --tool-current-report /tmp/2-tool-rerun-2026-04-29.json --tool-candidate-report /tmp/2-tool-rerun-2026-04-29.json --face-current-report docs/baselines/2c-recognition-pr.json --face-candidate-report docs/baselines/2c-recognition-pr.json --current-onnx models/object_detect.onnx --candidate-onnx models/object_detect.onnx --out /tmp/7c-eval-self.json
status_note: self-vs-self wiring verified end-to-end; face_delta=0.0 / tool_delta=0.0 / current_onnx_sha == candidate_onnx_sha. Confirms tools/shadow_eval.py --from-reports CLI is wired correctly and produces the expected zero-delta on identical inputs. Does NOT exercise a real candidate vs current comparison; that step is gated on producing a candidate ONNX (see next_steps).
why_run: Self-vs-self wiring smoke for the shadow_eval orchestrator: confirms parse_face_report / parse_tool_report / assemble_eval_deltas paths work end-to-end on real workspace fixtures, and locks the expected zero-delta shape so future candidate-vs-current runs have an unambiguous "no-op control" to A/B against. Also surfaces a schema clarification: --face-current-report requires an eval_pr.py output (per_bucket_at_default.western.{f1,n}); eval_distribution.py reports (e.g. 2-distribution-face-rerun-2026-04-29.json) are NOT compatible despite being labelled "face" baselines. The previously-stated half-unblock from the #2-face-rerun blob does not apply.
